query: give me details on the Anthropic models released and how that impacts SDLC
answer: Anthropic has had a busy release cadence in early June 2026. On June 4, they launched Claude Opus 4.8 — described as a near-generational leap in agentic performance — with stronger coding, task orchestration, and professional work capabilities at $5/$25 per million tokens. This was followed on June 9 by the dual launch of Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5, the most significant release yet. Fable 5 is a Mythos-class model with cybersecurity safeguards enabled, broadly available to Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise subscribers; Mythos 5 is the previously-withheld frontier model now released exclusively to Project Glasswing security partners, with the strongest documented cybersecurity capabilities of any model — capable of turning zero-day vulnerabilities into working exploits within hours. Both are priced at $10/M input and $50/M output tokens, less than half the prior Mythos Preview price. The SDLC implications are profound and immediate. On the development side, Anthropic demonstrated that Fable 5 migrated a 50-million-line Ruby codebase in a single day — a task that would have taken an entire engineering team over two months. GitHub noted it enables developers to "hand increasingly ambitious work to agents and trust the results across the software lifecycle." Anthropic also launched Claude Security (June 4), which scans enterprise codebases using frontier models, directly embedding AI into the security testing phase of the SDLC. The dual-use nature of Mythos 5 is the key risk to monitor: the same capability that accelerates defensive security testing also raises the bar significantly for adversarial threat actors, meaning engineering teams will need to integrate continuous AI-powered vulnerability scanning into their pipelines as a baseline hygiene measure going forward.
